title: Financial Literacy Game: Cash Crusade - The Hustle for Rands
summary: This project proposes the design of a mobile financial literacy game titled Cash Crusade: The Hustle for Rands, addressing the critical issue of financial illiteracy among South African youth - fewer than 30% of young people maintain a monthly budget and youth unemployment for ages 15 to 24 exceeds 60%. Through a review of existing tools such as 22seven, Fintr, and Cha-Ching, the project identifies a clear gap for a mobile-first, offline-compatible, and culturally localised financial education game for township and peri-urban youth aged 11 to 24. The proposed game uses choice-driven narrative mechanics, culturally grounded characters, and a warm South African visual identity to teach budgeting, saving, delayed gratification, and avoiding debt, set within environments like spaza shops, taxi ranks, and township markets. Deliverables include UI wireframes, character designs, and a moodboard. Future development would prioritise a minimum viable product, isiZulu and isiXhosa localisation, and partnerships with NGOs and government youth programs.
